← All worlds/World 13
World 13 · 2 states

Obsession

Films for the dark passenger — the love, the work, the idea that colonised you. The films here understand that obsession is just desire with nowhere to go.

Choose an emotion
Destructive LoveCreative Obsession
On Creative Obsession

At some point, the thing you loved became the thing you could not stop. The line between devotion and destruction blurred and then disappeared. You cancelled everything that was not it. You lost people to it. You lost sleep, health, perspective — and there were days you would have given everything and more. These films understand that the artist’s obsession is not a personality flaw to be corrected. It is a mode of being that comes with its own cost structure, its own private logic, its own version of grace.

Creative Obsession

Films for the work that ate you alive and still wasn’t enough.

10Curated films

10 films — quick browse

Black Swan

Darren Aronofsky · 2010

CS9.2

Whiplash

Damien Chazelle · 2014

🏆 Won 3 Academy Awards

All About Eve

Amadeus

CS9.2

The Red Shoes

Michael Powell & Emeric Pressburger · 1948

Adaptation

Florence Foster Jenkins

Ed Wood

CS9.0

Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters

Paul Schrader · 1985

CS9.0
2010

Black Swan

Darren Aronofsky

View Film →

A ballet dancer's obsessive pursuit of perfection for the lead role in Swan Lake drives her to the edge of sanity.

CS9.2
2014

Whiplash

Damien Chazelle

View Film →

A young jazz drummer endures an abusive conductor's brutal teaching methods in his obsessive quest to become great.

CS9.3
1950

All About Eve

Joseph L. Mankiewicz

View Film →

A seemingly devoted young fan insinuates herself into the life of a brilliant stage actress and begins replacing her with chilling, methodical precision.

CS9.4
1984

Amadeus

Miloš Forman

View Film →

Court composer Salieri narrates his descent into obsessive jealousy of Mozart's genius — the burning humiliation of recognising greatness you cannot possess.

CS9.2
1948

The Red Shoes

Michael Powell & Emeric Pressburger

View Film →

A ballet dancer is torn between love and the all-consuming demand of her art, embodied by an imperious impresario who will accept nothing less than total devotion.

CS8.8
2002

Adaptation

Charlie Kaufman & Spike Jonze

View Film →

A screenwriter tries to adapt an unadaptable book about orchid obsession and spirals into self-referential crisis — the film becoming his therapy and confession.

CS8.6
1996

Shine

Scott Hicks

View Film →

The true story of pianist David Helfgott, whose obsessive pursuit of musical greatness — encouraged and then crushed by a domineering father — leads to breakdown and eventual redemption.

CS8.3
2016

Florence Foster Jenkins

Stephen Frears

View Film →

The true story of a deluded socialite who was utterly convinced of her talent as a soprano — and who performed Carnegie Hall in 1944 to an audience that mostly could not believe what it was hearing.

CS8.8
1994

Ed Wood

Tim Burton

View Film →

The true story of Edward D. Wood Jr., considered the worst director in cinema history — and the most cheerfully, devastatingly obsessive filmmaker who ever lived.

CS9.0
1985

Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters

Paul Schrader

View Film →

A portrait of Japanese author Yukio Mishima, interweaving dramatizations of his novels with the events of his final day, when he died in a theatrical act of political obsession.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.
